title: zieria arborescens subsp. arborescens
intro: Zieria arborescens subsp. arborescens, also known as the Western Spotted Bush Peely-neck, is a shrub native to the coastal regions of eastern Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Zieria arborescens subsp. arborescens include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for internal use, or applied topically as a poultice or ointment to treat various health conditions.
